content.full_text:
Republican
National
Committee

Benjamin L. Ginsberg
Chief Counsel

Michael A. Hess
Janice P. Lacy
Deputy Chief Counsels

July 23, 1990

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: Advice Letter Request

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This is to request an advice letter pursuant to Cal. Code § 83114(b) concerning whether the Republican National Committee ("RNC") can establish a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee under 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18539.

The RNC is a national political party committee established and maintained by the Republican Party. The RNC seeks to establish a California political committee which would be considered a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee. Regulation 18539 expressly permits state and local committees to establish such committees, but is silent as to whether a national political committee may also do so. See §18539 (a)(1). While Section 18539 as adopted by the Commission does not expressly exclude national political committees, neither does it expressly list national political committees as entities which can establish Proposition 73 qualified recipient committees, and there is nothing to indicate that Proposition 73 intended to limit its scope to only state and local party units. It also should be noted that the term "political party" appears but is nowhere defined in California campaign finance law. See, e.g., Cal. Code §85302 and 85303(b).

Dwight D. Eisenhower Republican Center • 310 First Street Southeast • Washington, D.C. 20003 • (202) 863-8638
Telex: 701144 • FAX: (202) 863-8820

Accordingly, our specific inquiry is whether the RNC, as a national party committee, may establish a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee under Section 18539. If you have any questions, please contact me at (202) 863-8638.

Sincerely,

Benjamin L. Ginsberg

BLG:jd

sections.question: Whether the Republican National Committee (RNC), as a national party committee, may establish a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee under 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18539.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the Republican National Committee, as a national political party committee, establish a Proposition 73 qualified recipient committee under California regulation 18539, which currently permits state and local committees to do so but is silent on national committees?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the RNC to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section is present. Document contains clear QUESTION section. No section headers found as noted in parsing notes, but question content is clearly identifiable from letter body.
